title: Credit
what_it_is: Credit measures how trustworthy you look to lenders and gatekeepers.
how_to_raise: Pay debt down and avoid missed-payment spirals.
Keep monthly cashflow stable so crisis events do not stack.
how_to_lower: Carry too much debt or miss key bills.
Let crisis events pile up without stabilizing cash.
why_it_matters: Credit opens better housing and transport options.
Strong credit unlocks better debt relief situations.

title: Stress
what_it_is: Stress is the monthly pressure you are carrying from work, school, money, and life friction.
how_to_raise: Take high-pressure focus actions like overtime or promotion pushes.
Let housing, transport, or debt problems keep leaking into the month.
how_to_lower: Choose recovery-focused months and reduce unstable systems.
Improve housing stability, transport reliability, and cash cushion.
why_it_matters: High stress weakens performance and makes burnout more likely.
Stress works together with low energy to create collapse months.

title: Housing Stability
what_it_is: Housing stability tracks whether your living situation can absorb pressure without turning into a bigger problem.
how_to_raise: Stay current on payments and avoid repeated forced moves.
Use housing options that fit your budget and support system.
how_to_lower: Miss payments or sit in a fragile housing setup too long.
Take on rent pressure your current cashflow cannot cover.
why_it_matters: Low housing stability spills into stress and future events.
Stable housing makes recovery months actually work.

title: Transport Reliability
what_it_is: Transport reliability measures whether you can keep showing up without losing money, time, or momentum.
how_to_raise: Use reliable transport and avoid pushing weak vehicles too hard.
Keep enough liquidity for maintenance and repairs.
how_to_lower: Rely on breakdown-prone cars or underfund maintenance.
Ignore commute friction when your career needs better access.
why_it_matters: Transport problems can cost shifts, promotions, and mood.
Some career lanes need stronger transport access than others.

title: Career Momentum
what_it_is: Career momentum is how strongly your current lane is setting up promotions, income growth, and resilience.
how_to_raise: Pick focus actions that support performance and promotion progress.
Protect energy and transport access so you can perform consistently.
how_to_lower: Stay in downtrend months, transition badly, or stack instability.
Let stress and energy collapse undermine your work output.
why_it_matters: Momentum shapes promotion timing and career-specific opportunities.
Weak momentum makes layoffs and stall months more dangerous.

title: Education Standing
what_it_is: Education standing shows whether your current school or training path is stable enough to keep paying off.
how_to_raise: Keep stress manageable and energy high enough to hold the line.
Use intensities and monthly focus that fit your current stability.
how_to_lower: Stack exam pressure, re-entry drag, or cash strain without recovery.
Run low-energy, high-stress months while trying to push school hard.
why_it_matters: Strong standing keeps long-term career doors open.
Weak standing can slow or kill the upside of the run.
